file_name: SightLife Surgical, Inc. - STRATEGIC SALES _ MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason. Expiration or termination of this Agreement shall not limit any obligations of a party that were incurred prior to such expiration or termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with a 90-day written notice, at any time for any reason. Expiration or termination of this Agreement shall not limit any obligations of a party that were incurred prior to such expiration or termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict by requiring a 90-day written notice for termination, which contradicts the original clause allowing termination 'at any time for any reason' without notice. This adds a procedural requirement for termination not initially present. This introduces an in-text contradiction since the first sentence now requires a 90-day notice and the second sentence implicitly indicates that termination can occur instantly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with a 90-day written notice, at any time for any reason. Expiration or termination of this Agreement shall not limit any obligations of a party that were incurred prior to such expiration or termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for termination 'at any time for any reason', implying immediate termination. The changed text introduces a 90-day written notice requirement, which directly contradicts the immediate termination provision. While the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, the perturbation explanation clearly states how the introduction of the 90-day notice contradicts the original intent and creates an in-text contradiction. Therefore, the addition of the 90-day notice contradicts the original allowance for immediate termination.

perturbation.original_text: Surgical represents and warrants that Surgical's performance of all the terms of this Agreement will not (a) breach any agreement to keep in confidence proprietary information acquired by Surgical in confidence or in trust prior to commencement of this Agreement, or (b) breach any other agreement with any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Surgical represents and warrants that Surgical's performance of all the terms of this Agreement will not (a) breach any agreement to keep in confidence proprietary information acquired by Surgical in confidence or in trust prior to commencement of this Agreement, or (b) breach any agreement with any third party, unless said breach is related to a prior agreement that is considered by Surgical to be more advantageous than the current agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction and loophole into the warranty provided by Surgical. By adding the clause 'unless said breach is related to a prior agreement that is considered by Surgical to be more advantageous than the current agreement,' it creates uncertainty regarding whether Surgical is truly committed to not breaching prior agreements. This contradicts the initial representation and warranty that Surgical's performance will not breach any agreement with a third party. By adding this text, the clause becomes very ambiguous and can be used by Surgical to breach agreements with other parties, without breaching this contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Surgical represents and warrants that Surgical's performance of all the terms of this Agreement will not (a) breach any agreement to keep in confidence proprietary information acquired by Surgical in confidence or in trust prior to commencement of this Agreement, or (b) breach any agreement with any third party, unless said breach is related to a prior agreement that is considered by Surgical to be more advantageous than the current agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant loophole in Surgical's warranty, allowing it to breach prior agreements if Surgical deems them less advantageous than the current agreement. This directly contradicts the original warranty, which unconditionally states that Surgical's performance will not breach any agreement with a third party. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, which means the change has created an internal contradiction. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as it's unclear which agreements Surgical is bound to uphold. The explanation further highlights this contradiction. It falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, specifically when 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties,' leading to disputes over obligations.
